### Post by rui-902 on 2015-09-28
Hello,
my laptop has wireless, but a few days ago i lost ethernet and i don't know why ...

i'll post some outputs of commands and files to help you help me!

Thanks!
```

/etc/NetworkManager/NetworkManager.conf [main]
plugins=ifupdown,keyfile,ofono
dns=dnsmasq


[ifupdown]
managed=false

```

```

cat /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-bcm43.conf 
# Warning: This file is autogenerated by bcmwl. All changes to this file will be lost.
blacklist b43
blacklist b43legacy
blacklist ssb
blacklist bcm43xx
blacklist brcm80211
blacklist brcmfmac
blacklist brcmsmac
blacklist bcma

```

```

iwconfig 
wlan0     IEEE 802.11abg  ESSID:"Home network"  
          Mode:Managed  Frequency:2.422 GHz  Access Point: 00:05:CA:C4:A1:48   
          Retry short limit:7   RTS thr:off   Fragment thr:off
          Power Management:off
          
lo        no wireless extensions.

```

```

ifconfig only returns wlan0 and lo information , nothing more

```

```

sudo lspci | grep Network
02:00.0 Network controller: Broadcom Corporation BCM4313 802.11bgn Wireless Network Adapter (rev 01)

```

```

(portuguese sorry)

sudo lshw -C Network
  *-network               
       descrição: Interface sem fio
       produto: BCM4313 802.11bgn Wireless Network Adapter
       fabricante: Broadcom Corporation
       ID físico: 0
       informações do barramento: pci@0000:02:00.0
       nome lógico: wlan0
       versão: 01
       serial: e0:ca:94:3e:2c:47
       largura: 64 bits
       clock: 33MHz
       capacidades: pm msi pciexpress bus_master cap_list ethernet physical wireless
       configuração: broadcast=yes driver=wl0 driverversion=6.30.223.248 (r487574) ip=192.168.1.20 latency=0 multicast=yes wireless=IEEE 802.11abg
       recursos: irq:16 memória:f3200000-f3203fff

```

```

cat /etc/network/interfaces# interfaces(5) file used by ifup(8) and ifdown(8)
auto lo
iface lo inet loopback

```

```

cat /etc/NetworkManager/NetworkManager.conf 
[main]
plugins=ifupdown,keyfile,ofono
dns=dnsmasq


[ifupdown]
managed=false

```
